=============================================================================\n403\t\n404\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n405\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n406\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n407\t\tif err != nil {\n408\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\tdesc, err = nil\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// =============================================================================\n421\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n422\t// =============================================================================\n423\t\n424\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n425\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n426\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n427\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n428\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t// global tracker instance\n432\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n433\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n437\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n438\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n439\t//\n440\t// Parameters:\n441\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n442\t//\n443\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n444\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n445\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n446\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n447\t\n448\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n449\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n453\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n454\t// configuration issues.\n455\t//\n456\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n457\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n458\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n459\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n460\t\n461\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n462\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n463\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn result\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n469\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n470\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n471\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n472\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n473\t\n474\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n478\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n479\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n480\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n481\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n482\t\n483\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// =============================================================================\n487\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n488\t// =============================================================================\n489\t\n490\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n491\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n492\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n493\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n494\t//\n495\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n496\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n497\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n498\t//\n499\t// Error Type Validation:\n500\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n501\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n502\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n503\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n504\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n508\t//   - message: The error message content\n509\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n512\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n513\t//\n514\t// Example usage:\n515\t//\n516\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n517\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n518\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n519\t//\n520\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n521\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n522\t//\n523\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n524\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n525\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n526\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n527\t//\n528\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n529\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n530\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n531\t//\n532\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n533\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n537\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n538\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n539\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n540\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n541\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n542\t\n543\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n544\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n545\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n546\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n547\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n551\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n552\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n553\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n557\t\tif message == \"\" {\n558\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n559\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n560\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n561\t\t\t} else {\n562\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n563\t\t\t}\n564\t\t}\n565\t\n566\t\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n567\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n568\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n572\t\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n573\t\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n574\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n575\t}\n576\t\n577\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n578\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n579\t// instead of string-based error types.\n580\t//\n581\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n582\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n583\t//\n584\t// Parameters:\n585\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n586\t//   - message: The error message content\n587\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n588\t//\n589\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n590\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n591\t//\n592\t// Example usage:\n593\t//\n594\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n595\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n596\t//\n597\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n598\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n599\t//\n600\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n601\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n602\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n603\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n604\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n605\t\n606\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n607\t\tif message == \"\" {\n608\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n609\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n610\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n611\t\t\t} else {\n612\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n613\t\t\t}\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n617\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// =============================================================================\n621\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n622\t// =============================================================================\n623\t\n624\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n625\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n626\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n627\t//\n628\t// Parameters:\n629\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n630\t//\n631\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n632\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n633\t//\n634\t// Example usage:\n635\t//\n636\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n637\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n638\t//\n639\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n640\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n641\t//\n642\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n643\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n644\t//\n645\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n646\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n647\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n648\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n649\t\n650\t\t// Handle empty path\n651\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n652\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n653\t\t}\n654\t\n655\t\t// Parse and normalize the field \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n661\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n662\t\tif path == \"\" {\n663\t\t\treturn \"\"\n664\t\t}\n665\t\n666\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n667\t\tvar result []string\n668\t\n669\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n670\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n671\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n672\t\t\t\tcontinue\n673\t\t\t}\n674\t\n675\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n676\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n677\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n678\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n679\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n680\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n681\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n682\t\t\t\t} else {\n683\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n684\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n685\t\t\t\t}\n686\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n687\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n688\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n689\t\t\t} else {\n690\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n691\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n692\t\t\t}\n693\t\t}\n694\t\n695\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n696\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n700\t}\n701\t\n702\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n703\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n704\t\tif s == \"\" {\n705\t\t\treturn false\n706\t\t}\n707\t\n708\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n709\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n710\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n711\t\t\t\treturn false\n712\t\t\t}\n713\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n714\t\t}\n715\t\n716\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n717\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n718\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n719\t\t\t\treturn false\n720\t\t\t}\n721\t\t}\n722\t\n723\t\treturn true\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n727\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n728\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, FormatValidationErrorWithDetails functionality with a more ergonomic interface.\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tformatter := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t validationType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.expected = expected\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.actual = actual\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n// Context can include information like the endpoint being tested, operation type, etc.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.context = context\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithFieldName sets the specific field name WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n// If not set, suggestions will be auto-generated based on validation type and values.\n// Use this to override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// {\n\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\t}\n\n\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n\t\tPatternDetails: =============================================================================\n\n// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n//   - actual: The FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n//\n// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n// your specific requirements.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - validationType: The category of validation\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value received\n//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n//\t)\nfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n\tvalidationType string,\n\texpected, actual interface{},\n\toptions ...FormatOption,\n) ValidationError {\n\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n\tfor _, opt := range options {\n\t\topt(config)\n\t}\n\n\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual)\n\n\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n\t}\n\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n\t}\n\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n\t}\n\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n\t}\n\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn formatter.Format()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\ntype FormatConfig struct {\n\tContext            string\n\tResponseSnippet    string\n\tFieldName          string\n\tPatternDetails     string\n\tRangeInfo          string\n\tValidationDetails  []string\n\tSuggestions        []string\n}\n\n// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\ntype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n\n// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\nfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.Context = context\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\nfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\nfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\nfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\nfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\nfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\nfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\nfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n\t\tdesc = \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\ntype errorTypeTracker struct {\n\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n}\n\n// global tracker instance\nvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n}\n\n// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n//\n// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\nfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n}\n\n// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n// configuration issues.\n//\n// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\nfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\nfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n}\n\n// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\nfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n//\n// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n//\n// Error Type Validation:\n//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n//\n//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n//\n//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\nfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n\n\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n\t\tfieldNameStr = Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n//\n// Usage example:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: {\n1434\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1435\t\n1436\t\t// Add some tracked types\n1437\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n1438\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type2\")\n1439\t\n1440\t\t// Get the TestValidationFormatter_CustomSuggestionsOverride verifies that custom\n// suggestions override auto-generated ones.\nfunc TestValidationFormatter_CustomSuggestionsOverride(t *testing.T) {\n\tcustomSuggestions := []string{\n\t\t\"Check API endpoint configuration\",\n\t\t\"Verify service deployment\",\n\t\t\"Review network connectivity\",\n\t}\n\n\terr := TestFormatOption_Chaining(t *testing.T) {\n\terr := FormatCustomValidationError(\n\t\t\"multi_option_test\",\n\t\t\"expected\",\n\t\t\"actual\",\n\t\tWithContext(\"context 1\"),\n\t\tWithContext(\"context 2\"), // This should override context 1\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(\"detail 1\"),\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(\"detail 2\"), // This should add to details\n\t\tWithSuggestions(\"suggestion 1\"),\n\t\tWithSuggestions(\"suggestion 2\"), // This should add to suggestions\n\t)\n\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t// Last WithContext should win (context 2)\n\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, \"context 2\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected last WithContext to override previous value\")\n\t}\n\n\t// WithValidationDetails and WithSuggestions should TestValidationFormatter_EmptyValues(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tbuilder  *ValidationFormatter\n\t\tshouldPanic bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"empty expected value\",\n\t\t\tbuilder: NewValidationFormatter(\"test\").WithActual(\"actual\"),\n\t\t\tshouldPanic: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"empty actual value\",\n\t\t\tbuilder: {\n\t\t\tdefer func() {\n\t\t\t\tif r := recover(); r != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif tt.shouldPanic {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Expected to panic\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Unexpected panic: %v\", r)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}()\n\n\t\t\terr := tt.builder.Format()\n\t\t\tif err.Error() == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Error() to return non-empty string even with empty values\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASIC FormatError FUNCTION TESTS\n// consistent structure.\nfunc TestFormatError_BasicFormatting(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"status code error without field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"status_code\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected: *testing.T) {\n\tresult := FormatError(\"\", \"\")\n\texpected := \"[error] (no message provided)\"\n\n\tif result != expected {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, FormatError\n// produces consistent structure across different error types.\nfunc TestFormatError_ConsistentStructure(t *testing.T) {\n\terrorTypes := []string{\n\t\t\"status_code\",\n\t\t\"error_message\",\n\t\t\"content_type\",\n\t\t\"validation\",\n\t\t\"authentication\",\n\t\t\"authorization\",\n\t\t\"network\",\n\t\t\"timeout\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, errType := range errorTypes {\n\t\tt.Run(errType, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatError(errType, \"Test error message\")\n\n\t\t\t// Verify structure starts with [error_type]\n\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(result, \"[\"+errType+\"] \") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError(%q, ...) should start with [%q], got: %q\",\n\t\t\t\t\terrType, errType, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify message is included\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test error message\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError(%q, ...) should contain message, got: %q\",\n\t\t\t\t\terrType, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\t// Test with field name as well\n\tt.Run(\"with field names\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tresult := FormatError(\"validation\", \"Test error\", \"email\")\n\n\t\t// Should include field name\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"email:\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError with field should include field name, got: %q\", result)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Should still have consistent structure\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(result, \"[validation]\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError with field should start with [validation], got: %q\", result)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_SpecialCharacters verifies that FormatError\n// handles special characters in messages correctly.\nfunc TestFormatError_SpecialCharacters(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"newlines in message\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Line 1\\nLine 2\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"tabs in message\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"status_code\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Error:\\t404\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"unicode characters\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"error\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Error: ✓ ✗ →\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"quotes in message\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"message\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   `\"quoted\" and 'single'`,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatError(tt.errorType, tt.message)\n\n\t\t\t// Should not panic and should contain the message\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, tt.message) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() should contain special characters, got: %q\", result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Should have consistent structure\n\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(result, \"[\"+tt.errorType+\"] \") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() should start with [%q], got: %q\",\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.errorType, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_NoNilPanic verifies that FormatError does not panic\n// with nil or empty inputs and handles them gracefully.\nfunc TestFormatError_NoNilPanic(t *testing.T) {\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif r := recover(); r != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError should not panic, got: %v\", r)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\t// Test with empty strings\n\tFormatError(\"\", \"\")\n\n\t// Test with one empty\n\tFormatError(\"status_code\", \"\")\n\tFormatError(\"\", \"message only\")\n\n\t// Test with normal values\n\tFormatError(\"validation\", \"test message\")\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_WithValidErrorTypes verifies that FormatError validates\n// recognized ErrorType enum values and formats them correctly.\nfunc TestFormatError_WithValidErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"required error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"format error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"format\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid email format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[format] Invalid email format\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"range error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"range\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Value out of range\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"age\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[range] age: Value out of range\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"length error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"length\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"String too short\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"password\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[length] password: String too short\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"type error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Expected number, got string\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"count\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[type] count: Expected number, got string\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"value error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"value\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid value\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"status\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[value] status: Invalid value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"duplicate error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"duplicate\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Email already exists\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[duplicate] email: Email already exists\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"conflict error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"conflict\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Values conflict\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[conflict] Values conflict\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tvar result string\n\t\t\tif tt.fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tresult = FormatError(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tresult = FormatError(tt.errorType, tt.message)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify that valid error types are NOT tracked as invalid\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\tif len(invalidTypes) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Valid error type %q was incorrectly tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_WithInvalidErrorTypes verifies that FormatError tracks\n// unrecognized error types while maintaining backward compatibility.\nfunc TestFormatError_WithInvalidErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\terrorType      string\n\t\tmessage        string\n\t\tfieldName      string\n\t\texpectedOutput string\n\t\tshouldTrack    bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"custom error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:      \"custom_validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:        \"Custom validation failed\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:      \"field\",\n\t\t\texpectedOutput: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom validation failed\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:    true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"typo in error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:      \"requird\", // typo of \"required\"\n\t\t\tmessage:        \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:      \"email\",\n\t\t\texpectedOutput: \"[requird] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:    true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"unknown error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:      \"unknown_type_xyz\",\n\t\t\tmessage:        \"Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:      \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedOutput: \"[unknown_type_xyz] Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:    true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"HTTP-specific error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:      \"status_code\",\n\t\t\tmessage:        \"Status code mismatch\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:      \"response\",\n\t\t\texpectedOutput: \"[status_code] response: Status code mismatch\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:    true, // status_code is not a basic ErrorType\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"error_message type\",\n\t\t\terrorType:      \"error_message\",\n\t\t\tmessage:        \"Error message pattern not found\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:      \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedOutput: \"[error_message] Error message pattern not found\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack:    true, // error_message is not a basic ErrorType\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Reset tracking before each test\n\t\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t\t\tvar result string\n\t\t\tif tt.fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tresult = FormatError(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tresult = FormatError(tt.errorType, tt.message)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify output is correct (backward compatibility maintained)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expectedOutput {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %q, want %q\", result, tt.expectedOutput)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check if error type was tracked\n\t\t\tinvalidTypes := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldTrack {\n\t\t\t\tif len(invalidTypes) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected invalid error type %q to be tracked\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t\t} else if count, ok := invalidTypes[tt.errorType]; !ok || count == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type %q to be tracked with count > 0\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif len(invalidTypes) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected valid error type %q to NOT be tracked\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_ErrorTypeTracking verifies that the error type tracking\n// system correctly accumulates occurrences of invalid types.\nfunc TestFormatError_ErrorTypeTracking(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Reset tracking before test\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t// Use multiple invalid error types\n\tinvalidTypes := []string{\n\t\t\"custom_type_1\",\n\t\t\"custom_type_2\",\n\t\t\"custom_type_1\", // repeat to test accumulation\n\t\t\"custom_type_3\",\n\t\t\"custom_type_1\", // repeat again\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, errType := range invalidTypes {\n\t\tFormatError(errType, \"test message\", \"field\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Get tracked invalid types\n\ttracked := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\n\t// Verify counts\n\texpectedCounts := map[string]int{\n\t\t\"custom_type_1\": 3,\n\t\t\"custom_type_2\": 1,\n\t\t\"custom_type_3\": 1,\n\t}\n\n\tfor errType, expectedCount := range expectedCounts {\n\t\tif count, ok := tracked[errType]; !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type %q to be tracked\", errType)\n\t\t} else if count != expectedCount {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %q to have count %d, got %d\", errType, expectedCount, count)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify we tracked exactly 3 unique types\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 3 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 3 unique invalid error types, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_CaseInsensitiveValidation verifies that ErrorTypeFromString\n// handles case-insensitive matching correctly.\nfunc TestFormatError_CaseInsensitiveValidation(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Reset tracking before test\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\terrorType   string\n\t\tshouldTrack bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"uppercase REQUIRED\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"REQUIRED\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack: false, // Should be recognized as \"required\"\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"mixed case Format\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"Format\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack: false, // Should be recognized as \"format\"\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"lowercase range\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"range\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack: false, // Should be recognized\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"custom type uppercase\",\n\t\t\terrorType:   \"CUSTOM_TYPE\",\n\t\t\tshouldTrack: true, // Not a recognized type\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tFormatError(tt.errorType, \"test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\ttracked := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\t\t\tisTracked := false\n\t\t\tfor trackedType := range tracked {\n\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(trackedType, tt.errorType) {\n\t\t\t\t\tisTracked = true\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tt.shouldTrack && !isTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type %q to be tracked as invalid\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t} else if !tt.shouldTrack && isTracked {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type %q to NOT be tracked (should be recognized)\", tt.errorType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestTrackInvalidErrorType verifies the tracking function directly.\nfunc TestTrackInvalidErrorType(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Reset tracking before test\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t// Track some invalid error types\n\tcount1 := TrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n\tcount2 := TrackInvalidErrorType(\"type2\")\n\tcount3 := TrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\") // Track same type again\n\tcount4 := TrackInvalidErrorType(\"type3\")\n\n\t// Verify return values (accumulated counts)\n\tif count1 != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected first occurrence of type1 to return count 1, got %d\", count1)\n\t}\n\tif count2 != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected first occurrence of type2 to return count 1, got %d\", count2)\n\t}\n\tif count3 != 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected second occurrence of type1 to return count 2, got %d\", count3)\n\t}\n\tif count4 != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected first occurrence of type3 to return count 1, got %d\", count4)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify tracked types\n\ttracked := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\texpectedCounts := map[string]int{\n\t\t\"type1\": 2,\n\t\t\"type2\": 1,\n\t\t\"type3\": 1,\n\t}\n\n\tfor errType, expectedCount := range expectedCounts {\n\t\tif count, ok := tracked[errType]; !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error type %q to be tracked\", errType)\n\t\t} else if count != expectedCount {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %q to have count %d, got %d\", errType, expectedCount, count)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestGetInvalidErrorTypes verifies that GetInvalidErrorTypes returns\n// a copy of the tracking map to prevent concurrent modification.\nfunc TestGetInvalidErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t// Add some tracked types\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type2\")\n\n\t// Get the map\n\ttracked := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\n\t// Modify the returned map\n\ttracked[\"type1\"] = 999\n\ttracked[\"new_type\"] = 1\n\n\t// Get the map again\n\ttrackedAgain := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\n\t// Verify that modifications to the returned map don't affect the internal state\n\tif trackedAgain[\"type1\"] == 999 {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Modifying the returned map should not affect internal tracking state\")\n\t}\n\tif _, ok := trackedAgain[\"new_type\"]; ok {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Adding to the returned map should not affect internal tracking state\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify actual counts\n\tif count := trackedAgain[\"type1\"]; count != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected type1 count to be 1, got %d\", count)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking verifies that reset clears all tracked types.\nfunc TestResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Add some tracked types\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type2\")\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n\n\t// Verify we have tracked types\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 2 tracked types before reset, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\t// Reset tracking\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t// Verify all tracking is cleared\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 tracked types after reset, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\ttracked := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n\tif len(tracked) != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected empty map after reset, got %v\", tracked)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestInvalidErrorTypeCount verifies the count function.\nfunc TestInvalidErrorTypeCount(t *testing.T) {\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\n\t// Initially should be 0\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected initial count to be 0, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\t// Add unique types\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected count 1 after adding first type, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type2\")\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected count 2 after adding second type, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\t// Add duplicate - count should not change\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 2 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected count 2 after adding duplicate, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\t// Add third unique type\n\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type3\")\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 3 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected count 3 after adding third type, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n\n\t// Reset and verify count returns to 0\n\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected count 0 after reset, got %d\", InvalidErrorTypeCount())\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatError_BackwardCompatibility verifies that existing code using\n// FormatError continues to work without changes.\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FormatFieldPath FUNCTION TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestFormatFieldPath_BasicPaths verifies that FormatFieldPath handles\n// basic field paths correctly.\nfunc TestFormatFieldPath_BasicPaths(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tfieldPath string\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"simple field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"nested field path\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"user.email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"user.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"deeply nested path\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"data.user.profile.email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"data.user.profile.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"field with underscore\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"user_email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"user_email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"camelCase field\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"userEmail\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"userEmail\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldPath(tt.fieldPath)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldPath(%q) = %q, want %q\", tt.fieldPath, result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatFieldPath_ArrayIndices verifies that FormatFieldPath handles\n// array indices correctly in various formats.\nfunc TestFormatFieldPath_ArrayIndices(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tfieldPath string\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"array index with dot notation\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"array index at start\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"0.name\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"0.name\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"multiple array \"users.12345.name\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"users[12345].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"array index already in bracket notation\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"users[0].emails[1].address\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"nested arrays with dot notation\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"matrix.0.1.value\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"matrix[0][1].value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"array index in middle of path\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"data.users.0.profile.email\",\n\t\t\texpected: \"data.users[0].profile.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldPath(tt.fieldPath)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldPath(%q) = %q, want %q\", tt.fieldPath, result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatFieldPath_EmptyAndInvalidPaths verifies that FormatFieldPath handles\n// empty and invalid field paths gracefully.\nfunc TestFormatFieldPath_EmptyAndInvalidPaths(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tfieldPath string\n\t\texpected \",\n\t\t\texpected: \"(unknown field)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"whitespace with content\",\n\t\t\tfieldPath: \"  user.email  \",\n\t\t\texpected:
